events.title: Israel Bombs Lebanon
events.desc: Israel conducted strikes across Lebanon, killing at least nine people in the south. Raids also targeted a highway near Beirut. These attacks occurred despite a three-week-old ceasefire with Hezbollah, which has done little to halt daily exchanges of fire.

events.title: Israeli Strike Kills Seven in South Lebanon
events.desc: An Israeli strike on the town of Saksakiyeh killed at least seven people, including a child, and wounded 15. The Israeli military stated they struck Hezbollah militants planning attacks, and are reviewing reports of civilian harm.

events.title: Car Bombing at Pakistani Police Checkpoint
events.desc: A suspected car bombing followed by a firefight at a police checkpoint in Bannu, Pakistan, killed twelve people. Three injured personnel were taken to the hospital.

events.title: Car Bombing in Northwest Pakistan Kills Police Officers
events.desc: A car bombing at a police post in Bannu, Pakistan, killed at least three officers and was followed by an ambush. The police post was destroyed, and most of the officers on duty are feared dead.

events.title: Iran Threatens US Sites Amid Tanker Attacks
events.desc: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps threatened to target US sites in the Middle East and "enemy ships" if its tankers are attacked. This threat comes after US strikes on two Iranian tankers in the Gulf of Oman.

events.title: US and Iran No Closer to Ending War Amid Gulf Clashes
events.desc: The US and Iran remain far from ending their war, with recent exchanges of fire in the Gulf despite a ceasefire. The UAE also came under renewed attack. US intelligence suggests Iran could withstand a naval blockade for months.

events.title: UK Deploys Warship to Middle East
events.desc: The UK is deploying HMS Dragon to the Middle East for a potential multinational effort to protect shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The destroyer was previously in the Eastern Mediterranean to help defend Cyprus.

events.title: Israel Deports Gaza Flotilla Activists
events.desc: Israel deported two activists, a Spanish national and a Brazilian, after detaining them for participating in a Gaza-bound flotilla. Israel accused them of affiliation with a terrorist organization and illegal activity.

events.title: Settlers Force Re-burial of Palestinian Man
events.desc: Israeli settlers in the West Bank forced a Palestinian family to exhume their father's body from his grave near a settlement. The 80-year-old man died of natural causes and was buried with permits.

events.title: Lebanon Reports 39 Killed in Israeli Strikes
events.desc: Lebanon states that Israeli strikes have killed 39 people. Exchanges of fire between Israel and Hezbollah continue despite a recent ceasefire announcement.

events.title: Israeli Military Investigates Soldier's Act Against Statue
events.desc: The Israeli military is investigating a soldier photographed putting a cigarette in a statue of the Virgin Mary in Lebanon. This is the second incident involving a Christian figure in recent weeks.

events.title: Iran Accuses US of 'Reckless Military Adventure'
events.desc: Iran's Abbas Araghchi stated that the US engages in 'reckless military adventures' whenever diplomatic solutions are being considered, criticizing recent US actions.

events.title: Iran Demands Guarantees for World Cup Participation
events.desc: The Iranian Football Federation has presented FIFA with 10 conditions for participation in the World Cup, seeking guarantees amidst the ongoing geopolitical tensions.

events.title: Iran Considering US Proposal Amid Peace Hopes
events.desc: Mediator Pakistan reports that Iran is considering a US proposal, potentially leading to a permanent end to the war. This follows indications of fresh momentum towards a peace deal.

events.title: Fears of Renewed Gaza War as Hamas Disarmament Talks Stall
events.desc: Israeli media reports indicate that Israel is preparing to resume hostilities due to an impasse in disarmament talks with Hamas.

events.title: Satellite Images Show Likely Oil Slick Off Iran's Kharg Island
events.desc: Satellite images have captured a suspected oil slick spanning dozens of square kilometers near Iran's Kharg Island, raising environmental concerns amid the ongoing conflict.

events.title: Iran War Day 72: Israel Bombs Lebanon
events.desc: Israel killed at least 24 people in Lebanon in one day as Tehran's response to a US proposal to end the war is awaited.

events.title: Fearing Return to War, Iran Conservationists Shore Up Heritage Sites
events.desc: Conservationists in Iran are repairing historic sites damaged by the war with the US and Israel, though some repairs are expected to take years. Sites like Golestan Palace have suffered damage from shockwaves.

events.title: Efforts to Destroy Iran's Police Infrastructure
events.desc: Bellingcat identified at least 80 police stations or infrastructure related to law enforcement and the Basij paramilitary group that have been damaged or destroyed in the first three weeks of the war. The aim is to degrade the Iranian regime's repressive capacity.
